records.sprk_documentname: Master Services Agreement — Meridian Corp and Pinnacle Industries
records.sprk_filesummary: Master Services Agreement executed January 15, 2023 between Meridian Corp and Pinnacle Industries governing the supply of precision-manufactured components and technical collaboration. Contains Section 8 (Intellectual Property) requiring all IP developed under the agreement to vest in Meridian, and Section 12 (Confidentiality) with a 5-year non-disclosure period. This is one of the two primary contracts at issue in the litigation.
records.sprk_filetldr: MSA between Meridian and Pinnacle governing component supply and IP ownership rights, effective January 2023.
records.sprk_keywords: MSA, master services agreement, intellectual property, confidentiality, Meridian Corp, Pinnacle Industries, component supply, IP ownership
records.sprk_extractpeople:
Sarah Chen
Thomas Wright

records.sprk_documentname: Technology License Agreement — Precision Manufacturing Processes
records.sprk_filesummary: Technology License Agreement dated March 1, 2023 granting Pinnacle Industries a limited, non-exclusive license to use Meridian Corp's patented thermal compression molding process (US Patent 9,876,543) solely for manufacturing components under the MSA. Section 3.2 restricts use to MSA-authorized products only; Section 5.1 prohibits sublicensing or derivative works.
records.sprk_filetldr: Limited license for Pinnacle to use Meridian's patented thermal compression molding process (US Patent 9,876,543) exclusively under the MSA.
records.sprk_keywords: technology license, US Patent 9876543, thermal compression molding, non-exclusive license, restricted use, derivative works, Meridian, Pinnacle
records.sprk_extractpeople:
Sarah Chen
Thomas Wright
James Morrison
records.sprk_extractreference:
US Patent 9,876,543
TLA-2023-MC-PI-001
MSA-2023-MC-PI-001

records.sprk_documentname: Statement of Work No. 1 — Initial Component Manufacturing Run
records.sprk_filesummary: First SOW under the MSA, executed April 10, 2023, for Pinnacle to manufacture 50,000 precision-molded housings using Meridian's thermal compression process. Includes technical specifications, quality benchmarks, and a $1.2M fixed-price deliverable schedule over 6 months.
records.sprk_filetldr: SOW #1 for Pinnacle to manufacture 50,000 precision housings using Meridian's patented process at $1.2M fixed price.
records.sprk_keywords: statement of work, SOW, manufacturing, precision housings, thermal compression, deliverables, quality benchmarks
records.sprk_extractpeople:
James Morrison
Thomas Wright

records.sprk_documentname: Statement of Work No. 2 — Expanded Production Line
records.sprk_filesummary: Second SOW dated August 22, 2023, expanding Pinnacle's manufacturing engagement to include 120,000 additional units across three product SKUs. Incorporates modified thermal compression parameters developed during SOW-001. Total value $3.6M over 12 months.
records.sprk_filetldr: SOW #2 expanding Pinnacle production to 120,000 units across three SKUs at $3.6M over 12 months.
records.sprk_keywords: statement of work, expanded production, manufacturing, multiple SKUs, thermal compression, scale-up
records.sprk_extractpeople:
James Morrison
Thomas Wright

records.sprk_documentname: Statement of Work No. 3 — Specialty Alloy Components
records.sprk_filesummary: Third SOW executed January 8, 2024, for Pinnacle to manufacture specialty alloy components using an advanced variant of the thermal compression process. This SOW is significant because it required Pinnacle to develop tooling modifications that Meridian alleges were later incorporated into Pinnacle's AutoForge product line.
records.sprk_filetldr: SOW #3 for specialty alloy components involving tooling modifications later allegedly used in Pinnacle's AutoForge product.
records.sprk_keywords: specialty alloy, tooling modifications, AutoForge, thermal compression variant, manufacturing, SOW
records.sprk_extractpeople:
James Morrison
Thomas Wright

records.sprk_documentname: Amendment No. 1 to MSA — Extended Term and Revised IP Provisions
records.sprk_filesummary: First amendment to the MSA dated June 15, 2024, extending the agreement term by two years through January 2028 and strengthening IP ownership provisions in Section 8 to explicitly cover derivative processes and tooling innovations. Meridian argues this amendment was triggered by early concerns about Pinnacle's independent use of licensed technology.
records.sprk_filetldr: MSA amendment extending term to 2028 and strengthening IP ownership clauses covering derivative processes and tooling.
records.sprk_keywords: amendment, MSA, IP ownership, derivative processes, extended term, tooling innovations, contract modification

records.sprk_documentname: Amendment No. 2 to Technology License Agreement — Scope Clarification
records.sprk_filesummary: Second amendment to the Technology License Agreement dated September 3, 2024, clarifying that the license scope under Section 3.2 does not extend to independently developed products or product lines not specified in an active SOW. Pinnacle contends this amendment was not properly executed and lacks consideration.
records.sprk_filetldr: TLA amendment clarifying license does not cover independently developed products outside active SOWs; enforceability disputed by Pinnacle.

records.sprk_documentname: Termination Notice — Meridian Corp Terminates MSA with Pinnacle Industries
records.sprk_filesummary: Formal termination notice dated May 30, 2025, from Meridian Corp to Pinnacle Industries terminating the MSA and all active SOWs for cause under Section 14.2 (Material Breach). Cites Pinnacle's unauthorized use of licensed technology in the AutoForge product line as the basis for termination, with an effective date of July 1, 2025.

records.sprk_documentname: Complaint for Patent Infringement — Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries
records.sprk_filesummary: Complaint filed August 15, 2025 in the U.S. District Court, Northern District of California asserting infringement of US Patent 9,876,543 (thermal compression molding process) and breach of the Technology License Agreement. Alleges Pinnacle incorporated Meridian's patented process into its AutoForge product line without authorization. Seeks injunctive relief and damages exceeding $45 million.
records.sprk_filetldr: Patent infringement complaint alleging Pinnacle used Meridian's patented thermal compression process in AutoForge; seeks $45M+ damages.
records.sprk_keywords: complaint, patent infringement, US Patent 9876543, AutoForge, injunctive relief, damages, breach of contract

records.sprk_filesummary: Dr. Patel's supplemental damages report dated January 10, 2026, calculating Meridian's lost profits and reasonable royalty damages. records.sprk_documentname: Expert Report — Dr. Emily Nakamura: Patent Validity Analysis
records.sprk_filesummary: Dr. Nakamura's expert report dated January 5, 2026, opining that US Patent 9,876,543 is invalid as anticipated by three prior art references: a 2019 Japanese industrial patent (JP-2019-156789), a 2020 academic paper on thermal compression techniques, and a 2018 trade publication describing similar sequential pressure applications. Also concludes AutoForge does not infringe under either literal or doctrine of equivalents analysis.
records.sprk_filetldr: Dr. Nakamura's report concluding US Patent 9,876,543 is invalid based on three prior art references and AutoForge does not infringe.
records.sprk_keywords: expert report, Dr. Nakamura, patent invalidity, prior art, anticipation, non-infringement, doctrine of equivalents, Japanese patent

The report also addresses prior art references that were not considered in the original analysis.

records.sprk_documentname: Settlement Analysis Memorandum
records.sprk_filesummary: Confidential settlement analysis evaluating potential resolution ranges for the Meridian v. Pinnacle dispute. The memo analyzes comparable patent infringement settlements in the manufacturing sector, assesses Meridian's damages exposure, and models three settlement scenarios ranging from $2.5M to $8.5M. Recommends an initial demand of $12M with authority to settle at $6M.
records.sprk_filetldr: Settlement analysis models three scenarios and recommends an initial demand of $12M with settlement authority at $6M.
records.sprk_keywords: settlement analysis, damages, negotiation strategy, comparable cases, settlement range, confidential

records.sprk_documentname: Email: Defense Response to Production Schedule
records.sprk_emailsubject: RE: Meridian v. Pinnacle — Proposed Discovery Production Schedule
records.sprk_emailbody: Rachel, we reviewed your proposed schedule. We also have concerns about the scope of Request No. 14 regarding R&D communications.

records.sprk_documentname: Email: Motion to Compel Warning — Deficient Production
records.sprk_emailsubject: Meridian v. Pinnacle — Deficiencies in Pinnacle's Document Production
records.sprk_emailbody: David, we have identified significant deficiencies in Pinnacle's December production. Specifically, we received no engineering design documents for the AutoForge system despite Request No. 8 specifically targeting those materials. We also note the absence of any communications between Pinnacle's engineering team and management regarding the thermal compression features. If these deficiencies are not remedied within 14 days, we intend to file a motion to compel.

records.sprk_documentname: Email: Defense Response to Production Deficiency Letter
records.sprk_emailsubject: RE: Meridian v. Pinnacle — Deficiencies in Pinnacle's Document Production
records.sprk_emailbody: Rachel, we disagree with your characterization of the production as deficient. The AutoForge engineering documents are subject to a pending protective order request, and we have withheld them pending the Court's ruling. The internal communications you reference are protected by work product doctrine. We are prepared to discuss a reasonable resolution but reject the threat of a motion at this stage.

records.sprk_documentname: Witness List — Plaintiff's Proposed Witnesses
records.sprk_filesummary: Preliminary witness list identifying 12 witnesses plaintiff Meridian Corp may call at trial. Includes fact witnesses James Morrison and Thomas Wright, expert witnesses Dr. Robert Patel and Dr. Emily Nakamura, and corporate representatives. Each entry notes the witness name, affiliation, anticipated subject matter, and estimated duration of testimony.
records.sprk_filetldr: Plaintiff's preliminary witness list identifying 12 witnesses including fact, expert, and corporate representatives for trial.
records.sprk_keywords: witness list, trial witnesses, fact witnesses, expert witnesses, testimony, trial preparation
